ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for baked cheese tart; closest match is cheese tart (FOODON_03315091). Signature/branded variant not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Cheese cake) is the closest defensible category for a baked cheese tart; no dedicated baked cheese tart code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for baked cheese tart; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard baked cheese tart piece weighing approximately 80 g, as typically sold individually at Singaporean dessert and bakery outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Signature Baked Cheese Tart (Original) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Signature Baked Cheese Tart (Original), cr_id G00010479, with confidence 0.95. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 13.5g/100g based on the known composition of cream cheese, butter, and cream as primary fat contributors, consistent with USDA FoodData Central reference values for similar cheese-based pastry products.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake — Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Data (Atkinson et al., 2008)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Signature Baked Cheese Tart (Original). GI estimated at 45 (low) based on cheesecake analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283). Cheesecake entries with similar high-fat, moderate-protein, low-carbohydrate profiles cluster in the GI 40–50 range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 7.08) / 100 ≈ 3.2, using 80g serving size (8.85g carbohydrate per 100g × 0.80 = 7.08g).

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this item entirely; a single 80g piece delivers approximately 1023 mg sodium, approaching the full daily recommended limit of 2000 mg and far exceeding the 600 mg/100g threshold for high-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate (8.85g/100g) but high saturated fat and zero dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content (8.85g/100g) and high protein (16.09g/100g) support short-term glycaemic stability, but the high saturated fat (~13.5g/100g) and absence of dietary fibre may impair long-term insulin sensitivity; consume occasionally and pair with fibre-rich foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~13.5g/100g), critically high sodium (1279 mg/100g), and elevated cholesterol (78 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat, critically elevated sodium, and dietary cholesterol makes this item unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; limit to rare occasions and monitor overall daily saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~13.5g/100g and sodium 1279 mg/100g both exceed RA thresholds
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Extremely high saturated fat from cream cheese and butter promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6), and critically high sodium worsens joint and tissue swelling; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Signature Baked Cheese Tart (Original) is a premium individual-portion pastry sold widely in Singapore, popularised by Japanese-style cheese tart bakeries. It consists of a buttery shortcrust or tart pastry shell filled with a rich, creamy cheese custard made primarily from cream cheese, butter, eggs, and cream, then baked until the filling is set with a lightly golden, slightly soufflé-like surface. The texture contrasts a crisp, crumbly pastry base with a dense, velvety, mildly tangy cheese filling. Per 100g, it delivers 307 kcal, 16.09g protein, 23.06g fat (of which approximately 13.5g is saturated), 8.85g carbohydrate, 6.24g sugar, and a notably high 1279mg sodium driven by the cream cheese and butter content. Calcium is exceptionally high at 1375mg/100g from the dairy components. Dietary fibre is absent. Clinically, the critically high sodium makes this unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease, and the high saturated fat load is pro-inflammatory for rheumatoid arthritis patients. For type 2 diabetes, the low carbohydrate profile is relatively favourable in the short term, but the high saturated fat may impair insulin sensitivity over time. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low), consistent with high-fat, low-carbohydrate cheesecake analogues. Commonly consumed as a snack or teatime treat in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with a recorded confidence of 0.95 and review status 'reviewed'. The HPB record is an exact match for this specific branded product (cr_id G00010479). Saturated fat is estimated from known composition of primary ingredients and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central values for cream cheese and butter-based pastry products.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific branded baked cheese tart are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010479) or in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent product as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat value of 13.5g/100g is an estimate derived from the known fatty acid composition of cream cheese, butter, and cream as primary fat contributors, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cream cheese (FDC ID 173420) and butter (FDC ID 173410); this estimate is consistent with the health_suitability data present in the source record. Dietary fibre is confirmed zero per HPB source record. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on cheesecake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). Halal status is not confirmed; the item may contain non-halal ingredients depending on the specific brand and outlet.
